figures.caption: Table 15: MuSiQue bt effect (pp) by hop count. Iter-RetGen has no lobotomized condition. Bold blue = p < .05.

Table 16: Exhaustion gate (global best configuration, f_j0.6_u0.3_p2). ∆: score difference in pp. Fire: % of episodes where the gate triggers. Save: net token savings (%). Bold blue = p < .05. †Iter-RetGen (memoryless by design) has no lobotomized condition.
Table 7: Gap prevalence across baseline conditions. Each cell shows the count of episodes exhibiting the pattern.
Table 19: Exhaustion gate fire round distribution (global best config). Iter-RetGen always fires at round 4 (its maximum). †Pre-lobotomized.

Figure 1: The PBAI loop with our two harness interventions. The Belief State (blue) is updated by the Extractor and injected into the agent prompt. The Gate (orange) evaluates programmatic signals to detect stagnation.

Table 8: Full paired t-tests for persistent belief state (bt). Each cell reports the left condition minus the right condition in pp; positive means the left condition scores higher. Iter-RetGen has no lobotomized condition. Bold blue = p < .05.
Table 10: Token F1 and Exact Match: bt effect (pp). Iter-RetGen (memoryless by design) has no lobotomized condition. Bold blue = p < .05.
Figure 2: Pareto frontiers of quality vs. token efficiency across harnesses. Lobotomization (orange) collapses the frontier; bt (green) recovers much of it toward baseline levels (blue).
Table 11: ROUGE-1 and METEOR: bt effect (pp). Iter-RetGen has no lobotomized condition. Bold blue = p < .05.

Table 13: Exhaustion gate effect on string metrics (pp): blended (gate3 answer if triggered, natural otherwise) minus natural. Positive = gate improves the metric. Bold blue = p < .05. IRCoT benefits most on LoCoMo/MuSiQue; SWE shows negative F1 diffs because early stopping truncates verbose code answers that have high token overlap.
Table 12: SBERT and Rubric Judge: bt effect (pp). Iter-RetGen has no lobotomized condition. Rubric judge significance is from Table 8; SBERT significance is not shown.
